problem_spec.summary: For a two-frequency complex exponential curve with secondary frequency n+2 and discrete amplitude a=k/120, determine which (n,k) yield a simple curve (no multiple points) via a sharp sine-ratio threshold, then compute a weighted sum over all simple instances and reduce modulo 1000.
givens: For integers 0 \leq n \leq 50 and integers -200 \leq k \leq 200, set a = k/120 and m = n+2.
Define z_{n,a}(t) = e^{it} + a e^{imt} for 0 \leq t < 2\pi.
C_{n,a} is "simple" if there do not exist 0 \leq t < u < 2\pi with z_{n,a}(t)=z_{n,a}(u).
Define I(n,k)=1 if C_{n,k/120} is simple; otherwise I(n,k)=0.

invariant(s).name: simplicity_threshold_for_two_frequency_sum
invariant(s).statement: For integer m>=2, the curve t↦e^{it}+a e^{imt} has a multiple point iff |a|>1/m; hence it is simple iff |a|<=1/m.

invariant(s).name: discrete_k_threshold
invariant(s).statement: With a=k/scale and k integer, simplicity is equivalent to |k| <= floor(scale/m).

invariant(s).name: symmetric_k_cancellation
invariant(s).statement: For any integer M>=0, Σ_{k=-M}^{M} k = 0, so Σ_{k=-M}^{M} (n+k) = (2M+1)n.

essential_assumption(s): Multiple points are defined by equality z(t)=z(u) for distinct parameters t<u in [0,2\pi).
Since m=n+2>=2 for all n in range, no degenerate m=0,1 cases occur.
The k-range is wide enough that the active constraint comes from the simplicity threshold (here floor(120/2)=60<200).
uniqueness_certificate(s): The threshold |a|<=1/m uniquely determines I(n,k) for each (n,k) in the finite index set. Therefore S is uniquely determined, and its residue mod 1000 is unique.

technique(s).name: exponential_difference_to_sine
technique(s).purpose: convert the complex equality into a sine-ratio equation in x=(u-t)/2

technique(s).name: sine_ratio_upper_bound
technique(s).purpose: bound |sin(mx)/sin x| by m to obtain the necessary condition |a|>1/m
